content:
While it's a beautiful and nice thought that she goes out of her way, being such an old woman, for her grand child's medicine it might just be a futile effort.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 3:59PM
Brandon Yen
I honestly have many opinions about the symbolism used in this particular short story, and most are negative. can we really say the horn bush is a symbolic representation for her struggles when it was a quite literal obstacle for her because it caught up her dress and held her back. Her own struggles with eye sight at her advanced age makes it difficult for her to see what the obstacle is, but a literal obstacle is not quite a symbolic showing of the struggle she faces going back and forth on her path to and from the city to get her grandsons throat soothing medicine.

The scarecrow becomes an actually nice bit of symbolism in that in her great age she is thinking of death a lot. she was scared of the ghost and the scarecrow did it's job of being a decoy humanoid shape, scaring an old crow. She got herself worked up over it but once she realized what was actually in front of her she laughed and offered to dance with the scarecrow as it was flapping in the wind.
